title: erigeron thunbergii subsp. koraginensis
intro: Erigeron thunbergii subsp. koraginensis, also known as the Koragin Fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and flavonoid compounds. The Koragin Fleabane is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various skin conditions and injuries. Herbal preparations of Erigeron thunbergii subsp. koraginensis can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
